criteria.text: Acceptance criteria are clearly defined or the change is self-evident from the issue title
Implementation touches <=3 files in a single domain (no cross-cutting changes)
No new user-facing behavior is introduced (configuration, internal protocol, documentation-only)
No cross-cutting architectural changes (no shared interfaces, new abstractions, or multi-agent contracts)
No CE Gate scenarios are needed (no customer-facing surface affected)

scope_classification.default_rule: Default to full pipeline when any criterion is absent, the issue is ambiguous, or the scope is sparse. When in doubt, choose full.

user_override.text: Always present both tiers as options and recommend one - the user may choose either regardless of your analysis.

criteria.text: Severity is low - as rated by prosecution output (the only severity level below medium in the critical | high | medium | low schema)
Fix type is strictly mechanical: string literal changes, import path fixes, comment corrections, or formatting fixes - exhaustive list; no other fix types qualify
No logic changes: the fix does not add, remove, or modify any if/else/switch/loop/return/guard clause or conditional short-circuit (&&/|| guard reordering)
No test file cascade: the fix does not require changes to any test file or assertion
Changed value not in stored IDs or DB schema: backward compatibility is not at risk
Scope <= 1 file: the fix touches exactly one file

express_lane.revalidation_required: Tier 1 re-validation required after the specialist applies an express-lane fix.

post_fix_trigger.recursion_guard: This step is never triggered by another post-fix prosecution - only by a main review cycle (code review or GitHub intake proxy prosecution).

trigger_conditions.text: >=1 accepted finding was Critical or High severity
Any accepted fix modifies control flow
control_flow_changes: adding, changing, or removing if/else/switch branches
loop structure changes for for/while/forEach, including bounds, iteration variable, or break/continue

post_fix_trigger.skip_condition: Skip if no findings were accepted and applied (post-judgment: all REJECT or DEFERRED-SIGNIFICANT, no fixes applied).
evaluation_ordering: Condition 1 (severity) is evaluable immediately from the main-review judge output.
Condition 2 (control flow) requires the fix diff and is evaluated after Tier 1 re-validation and diff scoping.
If condition 1 does not trigger alone, proceed through Tier 1 re-validation and diff scoping before making the final skip decision.
